title: Stylus with thin tip for capacitive touchpads
abstract: The present invention relates to an electronic pointing device (101) for carrying out inputs (107) at a digital input device (102) with an electrically capacitively acting touchpad (108), wherein the electronic pointing device is electrically capacitively coupled to an electrical measurement signal of the touchpad (108), characterized in that the electronic pointing device (101) comprises at least one electronic circuit component (200) configured for modulating the electrical capacitance in the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An electronic pointing device (101) for carrying out inputs (107) at a digital input device (102) with an electrically capacitively acting touchpad (108), wherein the electronic pointing device is electrically capacitively coupled to an electrical measurement signal of the touchpad ( 08), characterized in that the electronic pointing device (101) comprises at least one electronic circuit component (200) configured for modulating the electrical capacitance in the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101 ). 2. The electronic pointing device (101) according to claim 1 , wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is additionally configured to carry out a modulation of the electrical capacitance of the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101), in the case of which a changeover between at least two capacitance states of the tip (104) is effected, and wherein at least one capacitance state assumes a capacitance value that lies below a predetermined capacitance value, e.g. below a capacitance detection threshold value of the touchpad (108) of a digital input device (102). 3. The electronic pointing device (101 ) according to claim 1 , wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is additionally configured to carry out a modulation of the electrical capacitance of the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device ( 01), in the case of which a changeover between at least two capacitance states of the tip (104) is effected, and wherein at least one capacitance state assumes a capacitance value that is equal to or higher than a predetermined capacitance value, e.g. a capacitance detection threshold value of the touchpad (108) of a digital input device ( 02). 4. The electronic pointing device (101 ) according to claim 1 , wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is additionally configured to carry out a modulation of the electrical capacitance of the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101), in the case of which a changeover between at least two capacitance states of the tip (104) is effected, and wherein at least one capacitance state assumes a capacitance value that iies below a 

 predetermined capacitance value, e.g. below a capacitance detection threshold value of the touchpad (108) of a digital input device (102), and wherein at least one capacitance state assumes a capacitance value that is equal to or higher than a predetermined capacitance value, e.g. a capacitance detection threshold value of the touchpad (108) of a digital input device (102). 5. The electronic pointing device (101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is additionally configured to carry out a modulation of a sequence of capacitance state changes such that capacitance states are changed individually or in groups. 6. The electronic pointing device (101 ) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is configured as a current-controlled negative impedance converter. 7. The electronic pointing device (101) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is configured to limit the magnitude of a countervoltage, generated for modulating the electrical capacitance of the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device, to a maximum value, so as to avoid undesirable electrical oscillations of the electrically capacitive coupling between the pointing device (101) and the touchpad (108). 8. The electronic pointing device (101) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the electronic circuit component (200) is additionally configured to carry out the modulation of the electrical capacitance in the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101) in time synchronism with a measuring frequency of the digital input device (102) at which contact position determination sensors of the touchpad (108), e.g. electrodes, are queried. 9. The electronic pointing device (101) according to one of the preceding claims, wherein the area of the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device which can be brought into contact with the touchpad (108) has mean diameters of less than 3 mm. 

 A method for carrying out inputs at a digital input device (102) with an electrically capacitively acting touchpad (108) by means of an electronic pointing device (101), comprising: receiving an electrical measurement signal of the touchpad (108) at the electronic pointing device (101), modulating the electrical capacitance in the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101 ). A system ( 00) for carrying out and recording inputs at a digital input device (102) with an electrically capacitively acting touchpad (108), comprising: a digital input device (102) with an electrically capacitively acting touchpad (108), an electronic pointing device (101) for carrying out inputs (107) at said digital input device (102), wherein the electronic pointing device ( 01 , 200) is electrically capacitively coupled to an electrical measurement signal of the touchpad (108) and the electronic pointing device (101 , 200) is configured for receiving an electrical measurement signal of the touchpad (108), and wherein the electronic pointing device (101) comprises at least one electronic circuit component (200), which is configured for modulating the electrical capacitance in the tip (104) of the electronic pointing device (101).
